Your video makes me want to speed up my visit. My father still has his close relatives there.

comments.author: @WendyeEricsen-Miller
comments.text:
I along with My Dad,Mom and 3Sisters lived in Lesotho 🇱🇸 
We were schooled in East London ..Eastern Cape with my Dear Old Grandparents as there were no white schools in what was then Basuto Land...It was a 
Beautiful Life...Quitting was in the south of Basuto Land ...
My Dad spoke Fluent Sesotho.
We as little Girls also learned to speak well...The Orange River We crossed in a rowing boat to get to an area where We would reach our family station wagon which was how We drove to the destination required. 
My Dad was a Trader Who had a Trading store where the local people would buy their produce.
My Mom picked fruit from the orchard My Dad planted...Then stewed the fruit and bottled it to sell in the trading store...
What a BEAUTIFUL LIFE it was at Phamong (the name of Our Trading Station....😢I dream about living There atleast once a week to this day!
